ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT PARKER HANNIFIN CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT PARKER HANNIFIN CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS DID NOT FILE AN OPPOSITION, ONE IS NOT REQUIRED. (SEE, E.G., WEIL & BROWN, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2010) AT 7:122.) PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT PARKER HANNIFIN CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT PARKER HANNIFIN CORPORATION'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO THE NEGLIGENCE, PRODUCT LIABILITY AND PREMISES LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS DID NOT FILE AN OPPOSITION, ONE IS NOT REQUIRED. (SEE, E.G., WEIL & BROWN, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2010) AT 7:122.) PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71. IT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, PRODUCT LIABILITY AND PREMISES LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Frances Barnes, Debbera Lee Cuthill, Robert Ray Barnes, Frank Clinton Barnes, James Henry Barnes, Cindy L. Salinas And Their Counsel For Failure To Comply With The Courts Order
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alex G. Taheri of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (415-766-3223) appearing for defendant / moving party Arvinmeritor, Inc.
Michael Eyerly of DEBLASE BROWN EYERLY LLP (310-575-9955), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Jay B. Barnes / Frances Barnes, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ARVINMERITOR, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFFS CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST ARVINMERITOR, INC. AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS FRANCES BARNES, DEBBERA LEE CUTHILL, ROBERT RAY BARNES, FRANK CLINTON BARNES, JAMES HENRY BARNES, CINDY L. SALINAS AND THEIR COUNSEL FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURTS ORDER

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Defendant ArvinMeritor, Inc.'s motion to dismiss plaintiffs' causes of action against it and for monetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's motion to dismiss plaintiffs' causes of action against defendant Arvinmeritor is denied. The Court granted defendant's request for sanctions per CCP 177.5 in the amount of $2,804. The requests for admission deem admitted. Plaintiffs ordered to respond without objection to the special interrogatories in 10 days from today's date. The Court further imposed sanctions of $900 paid by plaintiffs to the Court. The payments are due by 1/25 and the checks to be mailed out on 1/17. Counsel for defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Maria L. Bracken of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11741 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

******After the hearing the Court revised its ruling: The sanctions of $900 rescinded. Although the Court has the basis to impose sanctions under CCP 177.5 in view of the sanctions have already been imposed and the sanctions imposed currently the Court finds that sufficient. Counsel were notified by phone and defendant's to revise the proposed order.(503/TLJ)
